332: export class Factory<E extends Env = Env, P extends string = string> {
333:   private initApp?: InitApp<E>
334:   #defaultAppOptions?: HonoOptions<E>
335: 
336:   constructor(init?: { initApp?: InitApp<E>; defaultAppOptions?: HonoOptions<E> }) {
337:     this.initApp = init?.initApp
338:     this.#defaultAppOptions = init?.defaultAppOptions
339:   }
340: 
341:   createApp = (options?: HonoOptions<E>): Hono<E> => {
342:     const app = new Hono<E>(
343:       options && this.#defaultAppOptions
344:         ? { ...this.#defaultAppOptions, ...options }
345:         : (options ?? this.#defaultAppOptions)
346:     )
347:     if (this.initApp) {
348:       this.initApp(app)
349:     }
350:     return app
351:   }
352: 
353:   createMiddleware = <I extends Input = {}, R extends HandlerResponse<any> | void = void>(
354:     middleware: MiddlewareHandler<E, P, I, R extends void ? Response : R>
355:   ): MiddlewareHandler<E, P, I, R extends void ? Response : R> => middleware
356: 
357:   createHandlers: CreateHandlersInterface<E, P> = (...handlers: any) => {
358:     // @ts-expect-error this should not be typed
359:     return handlers.filter((handler) => handler !== undefined)
360:   }
361: }
362: 
363: export const createFactory = <E extends Env = Env, P extends string = string>(init?: {
364:   initApp?: InitApp<E>
365:   defaultAppOptions?: HonoOptions<E>
366: }): Factory<E, P> => new Factory<E, P>(init)

/**
 * @module
 * Factory Helper for Hono.
 */

/* eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any */
import { Hono } from '../../hono'
import type { HonoOptions } from '../../hono-base'
import type {
  Env,
  H,
  HandlerResponse,
  Input,
  IntersectNonAnyTypes,
  MiddlewareHandler,
} from '../../types'

type InitApp<E extends Env = Env> = (app: Hono<E>) => void

export interface CreateHandlersInterface<E extends Env, P extends string> {
  <I extends Input = {}, R extends HandlerResponse<any> = any, E2 extends Env = E>(
    handler1: H<E2, P, I, R>
  ): [H<E2, P, I, R>]

  // handler x2
  <
    I extends Input = {},
    I2 extends Input = I,
    R extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    R2 extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    E2 extends Env = E,
    E3 extends Env = IntersectNonAnyTypes<[E, E2]>,
  >(
    handler1: H<E2, P, I, R>,
    handler2: H<E3, P, I2, R2>
  ): [H<E2, P, I, R>, H<E3, P, I2, R2>]

  // handler x3
  <
    I extends Input = {},
    I2 extends Input = I,
    I3 extends Input = I & I2,
    R extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    R2 extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    R3 extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    E2 extends Env = E,
    E3 extends Env = IntersectNonAnyTypes<[E, E2]>,
    E4 extends Env = IntersectNonAnyTypes<[E, E2, E3]>,
  >(
    handler1: H<E2, P, I, R>,
    handler2: H<E3, P, I2, R2>,
    handler3: H<E4, P, I3, R3>
  ): [H<E2, P, I, R>, H<E3, P, I2, R2>, H<E4, P, I3, R3>]

  // handler x4
  <
    I extends Input = {},
    I2 extends Input = I,
    I3 extends Input = I & I2,
    I4 extends Input = I & I2 & I3,
    R extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    R2 extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    R3 extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    R4 extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    E2 extends Env = E,
    E3 extends Env = IntersectNonAnyTypes<[E, E2]>,
    E4 extends Env = IntersectNonAnyTypes<[E, E2, E3]>,
    E5 extends Env = IntersectNonAnyTypes<[E, E2, E3, E4]>,
  >(
    handler1: H<E2, P, I, R>,
    handler2: H<E3, P, I2, R2>,
    handler3: H<E4, P, I3, R3>,
    handler4: H<E5, P, I4, R4>
  ): [H<E2, P, I, R>, H<E3, P, I2, R2>, H<E4, P, I3, R3>, H<E5, P, I4, R4>]

  // handler x5
  <
    I extends Input = {},
    I2 extends Input = I,
    I3 extends Input = I & I2,
    I4 extends Input = I & I2 & I3,
    I5 extends Input = I & I2 & I3 & I4,
    R extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    R2 extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    R3 extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    R4 extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    R5 extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    E2 extends Env = E,
    E3 extends Env = IntersectNonAnyTypes<[E, E2]>,
    E4 extends Env = IntersectNonAnyTypes<[E, E2, E3]>,
    E5 extends Env = IntersectNonAnyTypes<[E, E2, E3, E4]>,
    E6 extends Env = IntersectNonAnyTypes<[E, E2, E3, E4, E5]>,
  >(
    handler1: H<E2, P, I, R>,
    handler2: H<E3, P, I2, R2>,
    handler3: H<E4, P, I3, R3>,

export class Factory<E extends Env = Env, P extends string = string> {
  private initApp?: InitApp<E>
  #defaultAppOptions?: HonoOptions<E>

  constructor(init?: { initApp?: InitApp<E>; defaultAppOptions?: HonoOptions<E> }) {
    this.initApp = init?.initApp
    this.#defaultAppOptions = init?.defaultAppOptions
  }

  createApp = (options?: HonoOptions<E>): Hono<E> => {
    const app = new Hono<E>(
      options && this.#defaultAppOptions
        ? { ...this.#defaultAppOptions, ...options }
        : (options ?? this.#defaultAppOptions)
    )
    if (this.initApp) {
      this.initApp(app)
    }
    return app
  }

  createMiddleware = <I extends Input = {}, R extends HandlerResponse<any> | void = void>(
    middleware: MiddlewareHandler<E, P, I, R extends void ? Response : R>
  ): MiddlewareHandler<E, P, I, R extends void ? Response : R> => middleware

  createHandlers: CreateHandlersInterface<E, P> = (...handlers: any) => {
    // @ts-expect-error this should not be typed
    return handlers.filter((handler) => handler !== undefined)
  }
}

export const createFactory = <E extends Env = Env, P extends string = string>(init?: {
  initApp?: InitApp<E>
  defaultAppOptions?: HonoOptions<E>
}): Factory<E, P> => new Factory<E, P>(init)

export const createMiddleware = <
  E extends Env = any,
  P extends string = string,
  I extends Input = {},
  R extends HandlerResponse<any> | void = void,
>(
  middleware: MiddlewareHandler<E, P, I, R extends void ? Response : R>
): MiddlewareHandler<E, P, I, R extends void ? Response : R> => middleware

import type { Context } from '../../context'
import { GET_MATCH_RESULT } from '../../request/constants'
import type { RouterRoute } from '../../types'
import { getPattern, splitRoutingPath } from '../../utils/url'

/**
 * Get matched routes in the handler
 *
 * @param {Context} c - The context object
 * @returns An array of matched routes
 *
 * @example
 * ```ts
 * import { matchedRoutes } from 'hono/route'
 *
 * app.use('*', async function logger(c, next) {
 *   await next()
 *   matchedRoutes(c).forEach(({ handler, method, path }, i) => {
 *     const name = handler.name || (handler.length < 2 ? '[handler]' : '[middleware]')
 *     console.log(
 *       method,
 *       ' ',
 *       path,
 *       ' '.repeat(Math.max(10 - path.length, 0)),
 *       name,
 *       i === c.req.routeIndex ? '<- respond from here' : ''
 *     )
 *   })
 * })
 * ```
 */
export const matchedRoutes = (c: Context): RouterRoute[] =>
  // @ts-expect-error c.req[GET_MATCH_RESULT] is not typed
  (c.req as unknown)[GET_MATCH_RESULT][0].map(([[, route]]) => route)

/**
 * Get the route path registered within the handler
 *
 * @param {Context} c - The context object
 * @param {number} index - The index of the root from which to retrieve the path, similar to Array.prototype.at(), where a negative number is the index counted from the end of the matching root. Defaults to the current root index.
 * @returns The route path registered within the handler
 *
 * @example
 * ```ts
 * import { routePath } from 'hono/route'
 *
 * app.use('*', (c, next) => {
 *   console.log(routePath(c)) // '*'
 *   console.log(routePath(c, -1)) // '/posts/:id'
 *   return next()
 * })
 *
 * app.get('/posts/:id', (c) => {
 *   return c.text(routePath(c)) // '/posts/:id'
 * })
 * ```
 */
export const routePath = (c: Context, index?: number): string =>
  matchedRoutes(c).at(index ?? c.req.routeIndex)?.path ?? ''

/**
 * Get the basePath of the as-is route specified by routing.
 *
 * @param {Context} c - The context object
 * @param {number} index - The index of the root from which to retrieve the path, similar to Array.prototype.at(), where a negative number is the index counted from the end of the matching root. Defaults to the current root index.
 * @returns The basePath of the as-is route specified by routing.
 *
 * @example
 * ```ts
 * import { baseRoutePath } from 'hono/route'
 *
 * const app = new Hono()
 *
 * const subApp = new Hono()
 * subApp.get('/posts/:id', (c) => {
 *   return c.text(baseRoutePath(c)) // '/:sub'
 * })
 *
 * app.route('/:sub', subApp)
 * ```
 */
export const baseRoutePath = (c: Context, index?: number): string =>
  matchedRoutes(c).at(index ?? c.req.routeIndex)?.basePath ?? ''

/**
 * Get the basePath with embedded parameters
 *
 * @param {Context} c - The context object
 * @param {number} index - The index of the root from which to retrieve the path, similar to Array.prototype.at(), where a negative number is the index counted from the end of the matching root. Defaults to the current root index.
 * @returns The basePath with embedded parameters.
 *
 * @example
 * ```ts
 * import { basePath } from 'hono/route'
 *
 * const app = new Hono()
 *
 * const subApp = new Hono()
 * subApp.get('/posts/:id', (c) => {
 *   return c.text(basePath(c)) // '/requested-sub-app-path'
 * })
 *
 * app.route('/:sub', subApp)
 * ```
 */
const basePathCacheMap: WeakMap<Context, Record<number, string>> = new WeakMap()
export const basePath = (c: Context, index?: number): string => {
  index ??= c.req.routeIndex

  const cache = basePathCacheMap.get(c) || []
  if (typeof cache[index] === 'string') {
    return cache[index]
  }

  let result: string
  const rp = baseRoutePath(c, index)
  if (!/[:*]/.test(rp)) {
    result = rp
  } else {
    const paths = splitRoutingPath(rp)

    const reqPath = c.req.path
    let basePathLength = 0
    for (let i = 0, len = paths.length; i < len; i++) {
      const pattern = getPattern(paths[i], paths[i + 1])
      if (pattern) {

    }
    result = reqPath.substring(0, basePathLength)

  basePathCacheMap.set(c, cache)

2:  * @module
3:  * Proxy Helper for Hono.
4:  */
5: 
6: import { HTTPException } from '../../http-exception'
7: import type { RequestHeader } from '../../utils/headers'
8: 
9: // https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc2616#section-13.5.1
10: const hopByHopHeaders = [
11:   'connection',
12:   'keep-alive',
13:   'proxy-authenticate',
14:   'proxy-authorization',
15:   'te',
16:   'trailer',
17:   'transfer-encoding',
18:   'upgrade',
19: ]
20: 
21: // https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9110#section-5.6.2
22: const ALLOWED_TOKEN_PATTERN = /^[!#$%&'*+\-.0-9A-Z^_`a-z|~]+$/
23: 
24: interface ProxyRequestInit extends Omit<RequestInit, 'headers'> {
25:   raw?: Request
26:   headers?:
27:     | HeadersInit
28:     | [string, string][]
29:     | Record<RequestHeader, string | undefined>
30:     | Record<string, string | undefined>
31:   customFetch?: (request: Request) => Promise<Response>
32:   /**
33:    * Enable strict RFC 9110 compliance for Connection header processing.
34:    *
35:    * - `false` (default): Ignores Connection header to prevent potential
36:    *   Hop-by-Hop Header Injection attacks. Recommended for untrusted clients.
37:    * - `true`: Processes Connection header per RFC 9110 and removes listed headers.
38:    *   Only use in trusted environments.
39:    *
40:    * @default false
41:    * @see https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9110#section-7.6.1
42:    */
43:   strictConnectionProcessing?: boolean
44: }
45: 
46: interface ProxyFetch {
47:   (input: string | URL | Request, init?: ProxyRequestInit): Promise<Response>
48: }
49: 
50: const buildRequestInitFromRequest = (
51:   request: Request | undefined,
52:   strictConnectionProcessing: boolean
53: ): RequestInit & { duplex?: 'half' } => {
54:   if (!request) {
55:     return {}
56:   }
57: 
58:   const headers = new Headers(request.headers)
59: 
60:   if (strictConnectionProcessing) {
61:     // https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9110#section-7.6.1
62:     // Parse Connection header and remove listed headers (MUST per RFC 9110)
63:     const connectionValue = headers.get('connection')
64:     if (connectionValue) {
65:       const headerNames = connectionValue.split(',').map((h) => h.trim())
66:       // Validate header names per RFC 9110 Section 5.6.2 (token syntax)
67:       const invalidHeaders = headerNames.filter((h) => !ALLOWED_TOKEN_PATTERN.test(h))
68: 
69:       if (invalidHeaders.length > 0) {
70:         throw new HTTPException(400, {
71:           message: `Invalid Connection header value: ${invalidHeaders.join(', ')}`,
72:         })
73:       }
74:       headerNames.forEach((headerName) => {
75:         headers.delete(headerName)
76:       })
77:     }
78:   }
79: 
80:   hopByHopHeaders.forEach((header) => {
81:     headers.delete(header)
82:   })
83: 
84:   return {
85:     method: request.method,
86:     body: request.body,
87:     duplex: request.body ? 'half' : undefined,
88:     headers,
89:     signal: request.signal,
90:   }
91: }
92: 
93: const preprocessRequestInit = (requestInit: RequestInit): RequestInit => {
94:   if (
95:     !requestInit.headers ||
96:     Array.isArray(requestInit.headers) ||
97:     requestInit.headers instanceof Headers
98:   ) {
99:     return requestInit
100:   }
101: 
102:   const headers = new Headers()
103:   for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(requestInit.headers)) {
104:     if (value == null) {
105:       // delete header if value is null or undefined
106:       headers.delete(key)
107:     } else {
108:       headers.set(key, value)
109:     }
110:   }
111:   requestInit.headers = headers
112:   return requestInit
113: }
114: 
115: /**
116:  * Fetch API wrapper for proxy.
117:  * The parameters and return value are the same as for `fetch` (except for the proxy-specific options).
118:  *
119:  * The “Accept-Encoding” header is replaced with an encoding that the current runtime can handle.
120:  * Unnecessary response headers are deleted and a Response object is returned that can be returned
121:  * as is as a response from the handler.
122:  *
123:  * @example
124:  * ```ts
125:  * app.get('/proxy/:path', (c) => {
126:  *   return proxy(`http://${originServer}/${c.req.param('path')}`, {
127:  *     headers: {
128:  *       ...c.req.header(), // optional, specify only when forwarding all the request data (including credentials) is necessary.
129:  *       'X-Forwarded-For': '127.0.0.1',

132:  *     },

135:  *     return res
136:  *   })
137:  * })
138:  *
139:  * app.all('/proxy/:path', (c) => {
140:  *   return proxy(`http://${originServer}/${c.req.param('path')}`, {
141:  *     ...c.req, // optional, specify only when forwarding all the request data (including credentials) is necessary.
142:  *     headers: {
143:  *       ...c.req.header(),
144:  *       'X-Forwarded-For': '127.0.0.1',
145:  *       'X-Forwarded-Host': c.req.header('host'),
146:  *       Authorization: undefined, // do not propagate request headers contained in c.req.header('Authorization')
147:  *     },
148:  *   })
149:  * })
150:  *
151:  * // Strict RFC compliance mode (use only in trusted environments)
152:  * app.get('/internal-proxy/:path', (c) => {
153:  *   return proxy(`http://${internalServer}/${c.req.param('path')}`, {
154:  *     ...c.req,
155:  *     strictConnectionProcessing: true,
156:  *   })
157:  * })
158:  * ```
159:  */
160: export const proxy: ProxyFetch = async (input, proxyInit) => {
161:   const {
162:     raw,
163:     customFetch,
164:     strictConnectionProcessing = false,
165:     ...requestInit
166:   } = proxyInit instanceof Request ? { raw: proxyInit } : (proxyInit ?? {})
167: 
168:   const req = new Request(input, {
169:     ...buildRequestInitFromRequest(raw, strictConnectionProcessing),
170:     ...preprocessRequestInit(requestInit as RequestInit),
171:   })

173: 
174:   const res = await (customFetch || fetch)(req)
175:   const resHeaders = new Headers(res.headers)
176:   hopByHopHeaders.forEach((header) => {

179:   if (resHeaders.has('content-encoding')) {
180:     resHeaders.delete('content-encoding')
181:     // Content-Length is the size of the compressed content, not the size of the original content
182:     resHeaders.delete('content-length')
183:   }
184: 
185:   return new Response(res.body, {
186:     status: res.status,
187:     statusText: res.statusText,
188:     headers: resHeaders,
189:   })
190: }

18: 
19: // 'default_content_type' is designed according to Bun's performance optimization,
20: //  which omits Content-Type by default for text responses.
21: //  This is based on benchmarks showing performance gains without Content-Type.
22: //  In Hono, using `c.text()` without a Content-Type implicitly assumes 'text/plain; charset=UTF-8'.
23: //  This approach maintains performance consistency across different environments.
24: //  For details, see GitHub issues: oven-sh/bun#8530 and https://github.com/honojs/hono/issues/2284.
25: const DEFAULT_CONTENT_TYPE = 'text/plain'
26: 
27: export const DEFAULT_OUTPUT_DIR = './static'
28: 
29: /**
30:  * @experimental
31:  * `FileSystemModule` is an experimental feature.
32:  * The API might be changed.
33:  */
34: export interface FileSystemModule {
35:   writeFile(path: string, data: string | Uint8Array): Promise<void>
36:   mkdir(path: string, options: { recursive: boolean }): Promise<void | string>
37: }

39: /**

44: export interface ToSSGResult {
45:   success: boolean
46:   files: string[]
47:   error?: Error
48: }
49: 
50: const generateFilePath = (
51:   routePath: string,
52:   outDir: string,
53:   mimeType: string,
54:   extensionMap?: Record<string, string>
55: ): string => {
56:   const extension = determineExtension(mimeType, extensionMap)
57: 
58:   let filePath: string
59:   if (routePath.endsWith(`.${extension}`)) {
60:     filePath = joinPaths(outDir, routePath)
61:   } else if (routePath === '/') {
62:     filePath = joinPaths(outDir, `index.${extension}`)
63:   } else if (routePath.endsWith('/')) {
64:     filePath = joinPaths(outDir, routePath, `index.${extension}`)
65:   } else {
66:     filePath = joinPaths(outDir, `${routePath}.${extension}`)
67:   }
68: 
69:   ensureWithinOutDir(outDir, filePath)
70: 
71:   return filePath
72: }
73: 
74: const parseResponseContent = async (response: Response): Promise<string | ArrayBuffer> => {
75:   const contentType = response.headers.get('Content-Type')
76: 
77:   try {
78:     if (contentType?.includes('text') || contentType?.includes('json')) {
79:       return await response.text()
80:     } else {
81:       return await response.arrayBuffer()
82:     }
83:   } catch (error) {
84:     throw new Error(
85:       `Error processing response: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error'}`
86:     )
87:   }
88: }

90: export const defaultExtensionMap: Record<string, string> = {
91:   'text/html': 'html',
92:   'text/xml': 'xml',
93:   'application/xml': 'xml',
94:   'application/atom+xml': 'xml',
95:   'application/rss+xml': 'xml',
96:   'application/yaml': 'yaml',
97: }
98: 
99: const determineExtension = (
100:   mimeType: string,
101:   userExtensionMap?: Record<string, string>
102: ): string => {
103:   const extensionMap = userExtensionMap || defaultExtensionMap
104:   if (mimeType in extensionMap) {
105:     return extensionMap[mimeType]
106:   }
107:   return getExtension(mimeType) || 'html'
108: }
109: 
110: export type BeforeRequestHook = (req: Request) => Request | false | Promise<Request | false>
111: export type AfterResponseHook = (res: Response) => Response | false | Promise<Response | false>
112: export type AfterGenerateHook = (
113:   result: ToSSGResult,
114:   fsModule: FileSystemModule,
115:   options?: ToSSGOptions
116: ) => void | Promise<void>
117: 
118: export const combineBeforeRequestHooks = (
119:   hooks: BeforeRequestHook | BeforeRequestHook[]
120: ): BeforeRequestHook => {
121:   if (!Array.isArray(hooks)) {
122:     return hooks
123:   }
124:   return async (req: Request): Promise<Request | false> => {
125:     let currentReq = req
126:     for (const hook of hooks) {
127:       const result = await hook(currentReq)
128:       if (result === false) {
129:         return false
130:       }
131:       if (result instanceof Request) {
132:         currentReq = result
133:       }
134:     }
135:     return currentReq
136:   }
137: }
138: 
139: export const combineAfterResponseHooks = (
140:   hooks: AfterResponseHook | AfterResponseHook[]
141: ): AfterResponseHook => {
142:   if (!Array.isArray(hooks)) {
143:     return hooks
144:   }
145:   return async (res: Response): Promise<Response | false> => {
146:     let currentRes = res
147:     for (const hook of hooks) {
148:       const result = await hook(currentRes)
149:       if (result === false) {
150:         return false
151:       }
152:       if (result instanceof Response) {
153:         currentRes = result
154:       }
155:     }
156:     return currentRes
157:   }
158: }
159: 
160: export const combineAfterGenerateHooks = (
161:   hooks: AfterGenerateHook | AfterGenerateHook[],
162:   fsModule: FileSystemModule,
163:   options?: ToSSGOptions
164: ): AfterGenerateHook => {
165:   if (!Array.isArray(hooks)) {
166:     return hooks
167:   }
168:   return async (result: ToSSGResult): Promise<void> => {
169:     for (const hook of hooks) {
170:       await hook(result, fsModule, options)
171:     }
172:   }
173: }
174: 
175: export interface SSGPlugin {
176:   beforeRequestHook?: BeforeRequestHook | BeforeRequestHook[]
177:   afterResponseHook?: AfterResponseHook | AfterResponseHook[]
178:   afterGenerateHook?: AfterGenerateHook | AfterGenerateHook[]
179: }
180: 
181: export interface ToSSGOptions {
182:   dir?: string
183:   /**
184:    * @deprecated Use plugins[].beforeRequestHook instead.
185:    */
186:   beforeRequestHook?: BeforeRequestHook | BeforeRequestHook[]
187:   /**
188:    * @deprecated Use plugins[].afterResponseHook instead.
189:    */
190:   afterResponseHook?: AfterResponseHook | AfterResponseHook[]
191:   /**
192:    * @deprecated Use plugins[].afterGenerateHook instead.
193:    */
194:   afterGenerateHook?: AfterGenerateHook | AfterGenerateHook[]
195:   concurrency?: number
196:   extensionMap?: Record<string, string>
197:   plugins?: SSGPlugin[]
198: }
199: 
200: /**
201:  * @experimental
202:  * `fetchRoutesContent` is an experimental feature.
203:  * The API might be changed.
204:  */
205: export const fetchRoutesContent = function* <
206:   E extends Env = Env,
207:   S extends Schema = {},
208:   BasePath extends string = '/',
209: >(
210:   app: Hono<E, S, BasePath>,
211:   beforeRequestHook?: BeforeRequestHook,
212:   afterResponseHook?: AfterResponseHook,
213:   concurrency?: number
214: ): Generator<
215:   Promise<
216:     | Generator<
217:         Promise<{ routePath: string; mimeType: string; content: string | ArrayBuffer } | undefined>
218:       >
219:     | undefined
220:   >
221: > {
222:   const baseURL = 'http://localhost'
223:   const pool = createPool({ concurrency })
224: 
225:   for (const route of filterStaticGenerateRoutes(app)) {
226:     // GET Route Info
227:     const thisRouteBaseURL = new URL(route.path, baseURL).toString()
228: 
229:     let forGetInfoURLRequest = new Request(thisRouteBaseURL) as AddedSSGDataRequest
230: 
231:     // eslint-disable-next-line no-async-promise-executor
232:     yield new Promise(async (resolveGetInfo, rejectGetInfo) => {
233:       try {
234:         if (beforeRequestHook) {
235:           const maybeRequest = await beforeRequestHook(forGetInfoURLRequest)
236:           if (!maybeRequest) {
237:             resolveGetInfo(undefined)
238:             return
239:           }
240:           forGetInfoURLRequest = maybeRequest as unknown as AddedSSGDataRequest
241:         }
242: 
243:         await pool.run(() => app.fetch(forGetInfoURLRequest, { [SSG_CONTEXT]: true }))
244: 
245:         if (!forGetInfoURLRequest.ssgParams) {
246:           if (isDynamicRoute(route.path)) {
247:             resolveGetInfo(undefined)
248:             return
249:           }
250:           forGetInfoURLRequest.ssgParams = [{}]
251:         }
252: 
253:         const requestInit = {
254:           method: forGetInfoURLRequest.method,
255:           headers: forGetInfoURLRequest.headers,
256:         }
257: 
258:         resolveGetInfo(
259:           (function* () {
260:             for (const param of forGetInfoURLRequest.ssgParams as SSGParams) {
261:               // eslint-disable-next-line no-async-promise-executor
262:               yield new Promise(async (resolveReq, rejectReq) => {
263:                 try {
264:                   const replacedUrlParam = replaceUrlParam(route.path, param)
265:                   let response = await pool.run(() =>
266:                     app.request(replacedUrlParam, requestInit, {
267:                       [SSG_CONTEXT]: true,
268:                     })
269:                   )
270:                   if (response.headers.get(X_HONO_DISABLE_SSG_HEADER_KEY)) {
271:                     resolveReq(undefined)
272:                     return
273:                   }
274:                   if (afterResponseHook) {
275:                     const maybeResponse = await afterResponseHook(response)
276:                     if (!maybeResponse) {
277:                       resolveReq(undefined)
278:                       return
279:                     }
280:                     response = maybeResponse
281:                   }
282:                   const mimeType =
283:                     response.headers.get('Content-Type')?.split(';')[0] || DEFAULT_CONTENT_TYPE
284:                   const content = await parseResponseContent(response)
285:                   resolveReq({
286:                     routePath: replacedUrlParam,
287:                     mimeType,
288:                     content,
289:                   })
290:                 } catch (error) {
291:                   rejectReq(error)
292:                 }
293:               })
294:             }
295:           })()
296:         )
297:       } catch (error) {
298:         rejectGetInfo(error)
299:       }
300:     })
301:   }
302: }
303: 
304: /**

309: const createdDirs: Set<string> = new Set()
310: export const saveContentToFile = async (
311:   data: Promise<{ routePath: string; content: string | ArrayBuffer; mimeType: string } | undefined>,
312:   fsModule: FileSystemModule,
313:   outDir: string,
314:   extensionMap?: Record<string, string>
315: ): Promise<string | undefined> => {
316:   const awaitedData = await data
317:   if (!awaitedData) {
318:     return
319:   }
320:   const { routePath, content, mimeType } = awaitedData
321:   const filePath = generateFilePath(routePath, outDir, mimeType, extensionMap)
322:   const dirPath = dirname(filePath)
323: 
324:   if (!createdDirs.has(dirPath)) {
325:     await fsModule.mkdir(dirPath, { recursive: true })
326:     createdDirs.add(dirPath)
327:   }
328:   if (typeof content === 'string') {
329:     await fsModule.writeFile(filePath, content)
330:   } else if (content instanceof ArrayBuffer) {
331:     await fsModule.writeFile(filePath, new Uint8Array(content))
332:   }
333:   return filePath
334: }

341: export interface ToSSGInterface {

344:     app: Hono<any, any, any>,
345:     fsModule: FileSystemModule,

355: export interface ToSSGAdaptorInterface<
356:   E extends Env = Env,
357:   S extends Schema = {},
358:   BasePath extends string = '/',

361: }

363: /**
364:  * @experimental
365:  * `toSSG` is an experimental feature.
366:  * The API might be changed.
367:  */
368: export const toSSG: ToSSGInterface = async (app, fs, options) => {
369:   let result: ToSSGResult | undefined
370:   const getInfoPromises: Promise<unknown>[] = []
371:   const savePromises: Promise<string | undefined>[] = []
372:   const plugins = options?.plugins || [defaultPlugin()]
373:   const beforeRequestHooks: BeforeRequestHook[] = []
374:   const afterResponseHooks: AfterResponseHook[] = []
375:   const afterGenerateHooks: AfterGenerateHook[] = []
376:   if (options?.beforeRequestHook) {
377:     beforeRequestHooks.push(
378:       ...(Array.isArray(options.beforeRequestHook)
379:         ? options.beforeRequestHook
380:         : [options.beforeRequestHook])
381:     )
382:   }
383:   if (options?.afterResponseHook) {
384:     afterResponseHooks.push(
385:       ...(Array.isArray(options.afterResponseHook)
386:         ? options.afterResponseHook
387:         : [options.afterResponseHook])
388:     )
389:   }
390:   if (options?.afterGenerateHook) {
391:     afterGenerateHooks.push(
392:       ...(Array.isArray(options.afterGenerateHook)
393:         ? options.afterGenerateHook
394:         : [options.afterGenerateHook])
395:     )
396:   }
397:   for (const plugin of plugins) {
398:     if (plugin.beforeRequestHook) {
399:       beforeRequestHooks.push(
400:         ...(Array.isArray(plugin.beforeRequestHook)
401:           ? plugin.beforeRequestHook
402:           : [plugin.beforeRequestHook])
403:       )
404:     }
405:     if (plugin.afterResponseHook) {
406:       afterResponseHooks.push(
407:         ...(Array.isArray(plugin.afterResponseHook)
408:           ? plugin.afterResponseHook
409:           : [plugin.afterResponseHook])
410:       )
411:     }
412:     if (plugin.afterGenerateHook) {
413:       afterGenerateHooks.push(
414:         ...(Array.isArray(plugin.afterGenerateHook)
415:           ? plugin.afterGenerateHook
416:           : [plugin.afterGenerateHook])
417:       )
418:     }
419:   }
420:   try {
421:     const outputDir = options?.dir ?? DEFAULT_OUTPUT_DIR
422:     const concurrency = options?.concurrency ?? DEFAULT_CONCURRENCY
423: 
424:     const combinedBeforeRequestHook = combineBeforeRequestHooks(
425:       beforeRequestHooks.length > 0 ? beforeRequestHooks : [(req) => req]
426:     )
427:     const combinedAfterResponseHook = combineAfterResponseHooks(
428:       afterResponseHooks.length > 0 ? afterResponseHooks : [(req) => req]
429:     )
430:     const getInfoGen = fetchRoutesContent(
431:       app,
432:       combinedBeforeRequestHook,
433:       combinedAfterResponseHook,
434:       concurrency
435:     )
436:     for (const getInfo of getInfoGen) {
437:       getInfoPromises.push(
438:         getInfo.then((getContentGen) => {
439:           if (!getContentGen) {
440:             return
441:           }
442:           for (const content of getContentGen) {
443:             savePromises.push(
444:               saveContentToFile(content, fs, outputDir, options?.extensionMap).catch((e) => e)
445:             )
446:           }
447:         })
448:       )
449:     }
450:     await Promise.all(getInfoPromises)
451:     const files: string[] = []
452:     for (const savePromise of savePromises) {
453:       const fileOrError = await savePromise
454:       if (typeof fileOrError === 'string') {
455:         files.push(fileOrError)
456:       } else if (fileOrError) {
457:         throw fileOrError
458:       }
459:     }
460:     result = { success: true, files }
461:   } catch (error) {
462:     const errorObj = error instanceof Error ? error : new Error(String(error))
463:     result = { success: false, files: [], error: errorObj }
464:   }
465:   if (afterGenerateHooks.length > 0) {
466:     const combinedAfterGenerateHooks = combineAfterGenerateHooks(afterGenerateHooks, fs, options)
467:     await combinedAfterGenerateHooks(result, fs, options)
468:   }
469:   return result

41:       beforeParentFlag = true
42:     } else {

44:       handleNonDot(path, resultPaths)
45:       beforeParentFlag = false
